_meta.title: UK Mortgage Lending Rules Reference Dataset
sources: Bank of England Financial Policy Committee LTV guidance
Major UK lender published product criteria (Barclays, HSBC, Lloyds, NatWest, Halifax, Santander, Metro Bank, Kensington, Precise, MT Finance)
_meta.notes: Multipliers shown are loan-to-income (LTI) ratios. Post-FCA MMR (2014), all lenders must stress-test at higher of notional rate (min 6.5%) or contract rate + 1%. High earner multipliers available only to applicants meeting full affordability assessment with verified income.

Barclays.tier_overview: Standard bank — strong for employed with regular bonus. Conservative on self-employed.

high.high_earner_notes: Upper multipliers require senior role + clean credit + no committed expenditure

bonus_treatment.note: Fixed bonus averaged over 2 years, 50% haircut applied

self_employed_rules.note: SA302 + 2 years of accounts required. Ltd company dividends treated as retained profit.

existing_debt.note: Monthly committed debt repayments deducted from gross monthly income before affordability calculation

btl_rules.note: Ltd company BTL requires 25% deposit minimum. Personal income can supplement rental shortfall.

HSBC.tier_overview: Strong on residential. Conservative self-employed — favour PAYE. BTL via HSBC Specialist Expatriate & International only.
HSBC.stress_rate_note: Uses 7% stressed rate for affordability. Less aggressive than some peers.

bonus_treatment.note: Regular bonus included at full value if verifiable via payslip. Subject to 12-month tenure minimum.

ot_treatment.note: Regular overtime included at full value if 12-month history. Requires payslip evidence.

self_employed_rules.note: HMRC SA302 + 2 years accounts. Income calculated as average of 2 years net profit.

btl_rules.note: BTL primarily via HSBC International / Premier. UK residential-only BTL limited.

Lloyds Banking Group.tier_overview: Covers Halifax + Lloyds brand. Most aggressive on multipliers for strong borrowers. Halifax most generous for first-time buyers.
Lloyds Banking Group.stress_rate_note: Stress at 6.5% or contract rate + 1%, whichever is higher.

high.high_earner_notes: Halifax upper-tier cases reviewed by underwriter — income multiples not automatic at max

bonus_treatment.note: Bonus averaged over 2 years, 33% haircut. Guaranteed bonus treated more favourably.

self_employed_rules.note: SA302 + 2 years accounts required. 2-year average net profit.

btl_rules.note: Both Lloyds and Halifax offer BTL products. Halifax BTL more accessible for portfolio landlords.

NatWest.tier_overview: Part of NatWest Group. Similar rules across NatWest / Royal Bank of Scotland brand. Strong for existing NatWest customers with banking history.
NatWest.stress_rate_note: Stress rate per FCA MMR — higher of 6.5% or contract rate + 1%.

self_employed_rules.note: SA302 required, 2-year accounts. NatWest more conservative on complex self-employed structures.

Santander.tier_overview: Strong on residential lending. More conservative on self-employed. Requires more documentation than some peers.

bonus_treatment.note: Bonus averaged over 2 years, 50% haircut. Guaranteed element treated at full value.

ot_treatment.note: Regular overtime included if 12-month history confirmed via payslip.

self_employed_rules.note: Strictly requires SA302 + 2 years accounts. More documentation than most.

Metro Bank.tier_overview: No existing banking relationship required. Fast decisions. No offset mortgage product. More conservative on self-employed.
Metro Bank.stress_rate_note: Metro Bank uses a stressed rate of typically 7% — more conservative than high-street peers.

bonus_treatment.note: Bonus averaged over 2 years with 50% haircut

self_employed_rules.note: Requires 3 years accounts (stricter than most). SA302 required.

btl_rules.note: Metro Bank BTL uses 130% rental coverage minimum — slightly more conservative than peers.

Kensington Mortgages.tier_overview: Specialist lender for complex income: self-employed, contractors, adverse credit, complex structures. Higher rates but more flexible.
Kensington Mortgages.stress_rate_note: Stress rate typically 7%+. Lower LTV deals available.

bonus_treatment.note: Specialist underwriting — bonus/overtime reviewed case by case. More flexible than high-street.

ot_treatment.note: Full inclusion if verifiable. Contractor day rate can be converted to monthly equivalent.

self_employed_rules.note: Can consider 1-year accounts for established self-employed. SA302 required. Contractor mortgage products available.

btl_rules.note: Kensington BTL can go to 80% LTV (higher than most). Portfolio landlord products available.

Precise Mortgages.tier_overview: Specialist lender for complex income and adverse credit. Conservative on standard residential; specialist for contractors and self-employed.

self_employed_rules.note: 1-year accounts accepted. Contractor products available with day-rate conversion.

MT Finance.tier_overview: Specialist short-term and bridging lender. Not a primary residential mortgage provider. Bridging / second charge focus.

basic.note: Bridging lender — not assessed by income multiplier

self_employed_rules.note: MT Finance provides bridging and short-term finance — assessed on security, not income multiples.

btl_rules.note: Short-term/bridging only — not a standard BTL mortgage lender
